content: Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM, RND, and curious agents) can be dynamically gated by a hybrid planning and learning system to allocate intrinsic reward only to novel state spaces that fall within the agent’s current episodic memory capacity, avoiding the common curse of unproductive curiosity where agents endlessly loop on noisy, unlearnable novelty. This hybrid gating framework prioritizes intrinsically motivated exploration that aligns with both the agent’s long-term planning goals and available memory storage.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
